reply_content: @TheActualJetFuel In the dictionary here's what the word "adaptation" means: "a film, television drama, or stage play that has been adapted from a written work.". Kindly take note that nowhere in the definition does it state it needs to keep everything the way it was in the material. As such, it can be modified. We see elements from every DMC game in this first season, which was clearly meant as an introduction: we meet Dante, Lady and Vergil, who, as all players know, they are very important in the story, so it gives the people who get into this franchise for the first time the chance to easily understand who the main characters are going to be. The Rabbit was taken from the DMC 3 manga, which is already established to be before the actual game takes place, so it makes sense why we got such a young Dante. Just because the show doesn't meet your personal criteria of what "adaptation" should be, doesn't mean it's terrible; it simply means you aren't open to exploring new ideas for this franchise.

I mean, when you consider the fact that this is partly an alternate continuity and partly a prequel to DMC3, as well as the fact that Reuben Langdon is retired now, it's oddly fitting that JYB would be voicing an even younger Dante.
It's also far from the first time this sort of thing has happened where two characters that are related share a VA for a younger version of them despite having different VAs for the adult versions. I honestly feel like this is the sort of thing most folks will forget about after an episode or two unless they really want to nitpick about it (and seriously, as this is a Netflix adaptation, I'm sure there will be something else that's far more concerning than a VA change that honestly can't be helped if the character's best known VA is now retired)
